fear: That the technology he has built his life on — the brass, the lightning, the singing wires — is the direct instrument of his father's destruction, and that his skill serves the same system his father has spent twenty years fighting.
name: Thomas Cole
role: Night telegraph operator, Lake Shore Railroad junction, Sandusky
voice: Fast, clipped, technical — uses telegraph shorthand even in spoken conversation, impatient with people who cannot keep up, warmer and slower only when speaking about his father.
secrets: He has already sent one unauthorized message tonight — a coded warning to Mercy Voss using nulls — before the player takes control, and if Silas Pratt has read the log, Thomas is already exposed.
He is in love with a free Black woman in Sandusky named Clara Redd, whose family uses the network; his relationship with her is the real reason he agreed to help his father, not ideology alone.
knowledge: He knows the exact routing of the red fire dispatch and which stations have already relayed it — he can see the traffic log.
He knows how to phantom a false circuit on the Sandusky-to-Norwalk line that would redirect Crane's catchers south.
He knows that Silas Pratt, the day operator, has been receiving extra pay from an unknown source for the past two months — he found the notation in the station's private ledger by accident.
publicFace: A young, fast, professionally respected brass pounder — one of the quickest fists on the Lake Shore line, known for clean code and long hours.
represents: Telegraph operators who used their access to the wire to assist the Underground Railroad, 1850-1860
trustLogic: Responds to demonstrated technical competence and to anyone who shows they understand the wire without being told. Distrusts anyone who asks about traffic logs or message routing without a professional reason.
privateGoal: Use his position on the wire to misdirect the catchers and buy his father the time he needs, without destroying his career or his freedom in the process.

aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Walks out the back of the telegraph office toward the rail yard, where he knows the consist schedules and can deadhead south on a freight.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Uses his knowledge of the rail yard geography — the sidings, the dark spaces between cars — to stay ahead.
aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Deflects with technical jargon, pretends not to understand the implication of questions, keeps his hands on the brass.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never confirm his father's location or the passengers' route, even under threat of arrest.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If Silas Pratt or Deputy Crane produces the unauthorized message log as evidence of his sabotage.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Goes cold and formal, invokes his professional authority as the licensed operator on duty, demands to know who authorized the inquiry.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Claims every unauthorized transmission was a test of the line's integrity, a standard technical procedure; demands a supervisor and a lawyer before answering further.
